title: gh#87: nginx: add real_ip module support so rate limits + audit logs key on real client IP behind layered proxies
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/87.
Backlog section: Bugs.

## Context

Surfaced in [Codex review on #86](https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/pull/86#discussion_r0). When the production nginx in this repo sits behind another TLS-terminating proxy (Cloudflare Tunnel, Caddy, Traefik, or a custom LAN nginx — see the v1.0.1 upgrade notes), two related problems show up:

### Problem 1 — Rate-limit bucket sharing

`nginx/nginx.conf` lines 27-28:

```nginx
limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=api_per_ip:10m rate=10r/s;
limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=admin_per_ip:10m rate=2r/s;
```

`$binary_remote_addr` is the **immediate** peer — whoever opened the TCP connection. Behind an upstream proxy, every real client appears to come from the same upstream-proxy IP, so all users behind that proxy share a single 10r/s bucket on `/api/` and 2r/s bucket on `/admin/`. A loud user (or accidental client loop) can starve everyone else; a DoS attempt from one cooperating client trivially exhausts the bucket for the whole community.

### Problem 2 — Lost client IP for audit logs

`nginx/nginx.conf` lines 67, 77, 90, 101:

```nginx
proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $remote_addr;
```

This **overwrites** any incoming XFF chain with `$remote_addr` (the upstream proxy's address). `req.ip` in Express resolves to the upstream proxy. Backend audit logs for failed logins, profile changes, etc. lose the real client IP. The chain-overwrite was a deliberate choice in #50 to avoid trusting spoofed XFF — but the right way to get trust + real client IP is the real_ip module, not chain-replacement.

## Fix sketch

Use [`ngx_http_realip_module`](https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_realip_module.html):

```nginx
set_real_ip_from ${NGINX_TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDR};   # e.g. 172.17.0.0/16 for Docker bridge, or Cloudflare ranges
real_ip_header X-Forwarded-For;
real_ip_recursive on;
```

When the connection comes from a trusted source, nginx pulls the real client IP out of the XFF chain (recursively, picking the leftmost untrusted address) and replaces `$remote_addr` for the request. Knock-on effects:

- `$binary_remote_addr` in `limit_req_zone` resolves to the real client → per-real-client rate limiting works
- `$remote_addr` in `proxy_set_header X-Real-IP` / `X-Forwarded-For` carries the real client
- Connections from outside the trusted CIDR fall back to current behaviour (no XFF trust)

### Why this needs to be configurable

The trusted-upstream CIDR is environment-specific:
- Custom LAN nginx → typically `10.0.0.0/8` or `192.168.0.0/16`
- Caddy/Traefik on same host → loopback `127.0.0.1/32`
- Cloudflare Tunnel → [Cloudflare's published IP ranges](https://www.cloudflare.com/ips/) (this list rotates)
- No upstream proxy → empty (default, current behaviour)

Two viable shapes:

**Shape A — env-var-driven template.** `nginx.conf.template` with `${NGINX_TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDR}` (multi-value, space-separated). Docker entrypoint runs `envsubst` once at container start. Empty default = current behaviour, no trust.

**Shape B — separate `realip.conf` snippet.** Operator drops a file with their `set_real_ip_from` lines into a mounted `conf.d/` directory. nginx `include`s `/etc/nginx/realip.conf` from the main http block. No template substitution; operator writes nginx syntax directly.

A is more discoverable (one env var to set); B is more flexible (multi-line CIDR lists, comments). A is probably the right call for this single-developer self-host context.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `NGINX_TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDR` env var (or equivalent) configures the trusted upstream
- [ ] When set, `$binary_remote_addr` for rate-limit zones resolves to the real client IP
- [ ] When set, `X-Real-IP` / `X-Forwarded-For` headers passed to the API carry the real client
- [ ] When unset (default), behaviour matches current v1.0.1 (no XFF trust)
- [ ] CHANGELOG upgrade notes describe how to configure for each common upstream-proxy type
- [ ] Test (integration or middleware-level) asserts a forged `X-Forwarded-For` from outside the trusted CIDR is **not** honoured

## Out of scope

- Per-route per-user rate limiting (this is for per-IP only)
- Cloudflare-specific automated CIDR refresh (operators can pin a known-good list)
- Replacing `limit_req` with a DB-backed rate limiter

## Notes

Surfaced by Codex in PR #86 review. Flagging rather than fixing inline because v1.0.1 should ship the existing bug fixes promptly, and the proper fix needs configuration design + integration testing that warrants its own PR. Current behaviour is no worse than pre-#25 for single-tier deployments; for layered-proxy deployments, the v1.0.1 upgrade notes document the limitation.

history.reasoning: Original evidence path still exists at nginx/nginx.conf. The relevant lines moved/expanded but the behavior remains: rate-limit zones still key on $binary_remote_addr at nginx/nginx.conf:35-36, and the production proxy locations still set X-Real-IP and X-Forwarded-For to $remote_addr at nginx/nginx.conf:84-85, 94-95, 107-108, and 118-119. No real_ip support is present: repository-wide search found no active set_real_ip_from, real_ip_header, real_ip_recursive, or NGINX_TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDR configuration. Current tests also pin the old behavior in packages/api/src/__tests__/middleware.test.ts:135-146 and 214-219 by expecting X-Forwarded-For $remote_addr and $binary_remote_addr rate-limit zones. CHANGELOG.md:129-137 and BACKLOG.md:111 explicitly describe #87 as still tracked/open. I attempted the focused middleware test, but it could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vitest tried to write .vite-temp and failed with EPERM; the checked-in test source still provides evidence that the current expected behavior is the unfixed behavior.
commands: rg -n "NGINX_TRUSTED|real_ip|set_real_ip_from|real_ip_header|real_ip_recursive|X-Forwarded-For|binary_remote_addr" docker-compose.yml docker-compose.dev.yml Dockerfile nginx packages docs CHANGELOG.md BACKLOG.md

history.reasoning: Original evidence path nginx/nginx.conf still exists, but the behavior has changed. The production nginx config now includes /tmp/nginx-realip/*.conf before the limit_req_zone declarations, so $binary_remote_addr is evaluated after real_ip processing when configured. docker-compose.yml now exposes NGINX_TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDR to nginx, Dockerfile installs nginx/realip-env.sh into /docker-entrypoint.d, and nginx/realip-env.sh generates set_real_ip_from entries plus real_ip_header X-Forwarded-For and real_ip_recursive on only when the env var is non-empty, preserving the default no-XFF-trust behavior. The proxy locations still set X-Real-IP and X-Forwarded-For to $remote_addr, but that is now the resolved client IP when real_ip trusts the upstream, and remains the immediate peer otherwise. Tests were updated to assert the include ordering, env passthrough, generated real_ip config, and Express trust-proxy rejection of untrusted LAN sources; the focused Vitest command could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write packages/api/node_modules/.vite-temp and failed with EPERM. BACKLOG.md still lists gh#87 as open, but that appears stale relative to the current code and changelog.
commands: rg -n "real_ip|set_real_ip_from|real_ip_header|real_ip_recursive|NGINX_TRUSTED|binary_remote_addr|X-Forwarded-For|X-Real-IP|limit_req_zone" nginx packages docs CHANGELOG.md BACKLOG.md docker-compose.yml docker-compose.dev.yml Dockerfile 2>/dev/null

rg -n "nginx overwrites|generates real_ip|trusted_proxy|real_ip|NGINX_TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDR|X-Forwarded-For" packages/api/src/__tests__ packages/api/src nginx Dockerfile docker-compose.yml CHANGELOG.md BACKLOG.md
git diff -- nginx/nginx.conf nginx/realip-env.sh Dockerfile docker-compose.yml packages/api/src/__tests__/middleware.test.ts CHANGELOG.md | sed -n '1,260p'
